title: peraxilla punctata
intro: Peraxilla punctata, also known as the Kawakawa or New Zealand pepper tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand. The leaves of the Peraxilla punctata plant have been used in traditional Maori medicine for centuries, exhibiting potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Peraxilla punctata include reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and treating various skin conditions. Peraxilla punctata is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be applied topically as a cream or oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
